Definition.text: Mark is a Curse subset mechanic that applies a unique curse state and tracks one marked target per source by default.

Purpose.text: Create persistent marked-target state for curse interactions and reward behavior.

Core Rules.text: A new Mark replaces prior Mark from the same source and is non-stackable by design.

Internal Rules.text: Mark state is removed when marker dies or source conditions invalidate the state.

Calculation Order.text: 1) skill hits valid target 2) check existing Mark from same source 3) replace previous mark 4) apply new Mark state 5) resolve curse limits and conflicts.

Priority Rules.text: Per-source replacement is strongest rule: latest valid Mark replaces prior Mark state.

Preconditions.text: A valid Mark source must resolve on an eligible enemy target and valid source ownership.

Postconditions.text: Target has Marked state with active curse effects until source-target lifecycle ends.

Limitations.text: One Mark from the same source is active at a time, with history-sensitive permanence and removal conditions.

Caps.text: Mark target cap is one per source owner; additional applications replace existing Mark.

Formula.text: No arithmetic formula across all Mark; state is replacement-based with curse-effect metadata.

prevents: multiple simultaneous marks from same source
cannot_affect: adding a second Mark from same source without replacement

Conflicts.text: Mark and Hex share curse-limit behavior; Mark state can be constrained by shared curse infrastructure.

Exceptions.text: Patch history changed Mark from temporary to permanent and changed behavior on source/target death and distance.

Hidden Rules.text: Mark may not display duration as a curse because status became permanent in 3.17.0.

Developer Notes.text: Represent Mark as state replacement machine rather than timed aura-like effect.

Historical Changes.text: 3.17.0 made Mark curse skills permanent and removed Duration tag; 3.19.1 removed monster marks at long distance.

text.note: Some curse skills were reworked into Mark as a subset.
Mark curse skills became permanent and lost the Duration tag; applied marks removed when marker dies.
Marks applied by monsters are removed if target moves far away.

text: Applying a second Mark from same source replaces the previous Mark.
Monster-applied Mark is removed when the monster dies.
Mark is not the same as generic Hex duration logic.
Mark is generally permanent for source-owner state until removal condition.

prevents: multiple simultaneous marks by same source
destroys: Mark on marker death or source loss
removes: Mark after marker/target removal or distance condition
